summary: Two KB-lifecycle features for Fabric (pcf), telemetry + cross-repo research driven: (F1) a content-age credibility decay MULTIPLIER wired into recall scoring — fills the content-age gap that doctor's usage-inactivity decay does not cover; (F2) suggestRelatedEdges auto-edge-suggestion — the render path for related_appended is already live, the real gap is empty source `related:[]`, so we generate advisory edge suggestions routed through the fabric-review human-gated write path (NOT the read/archive hot path).
approach: F1 is a 2-task vertical: TASK-001 lands the shared config + zod schema (per-type half-lives, per-maturity floor, score_breakdown.credibility component) and rebuilds fabric-shared dist; TASK-002 wires the multiplier at the single insertion point plan-context.ts:1528 with an EXACT mirror at :1585 (final===score invariant) driven by created_at age. F2 is a 2-task vertical parallel to F1: TASK-003 adds the pure suggestRelatedEdges(nodes) function beside buildRelatedGraph reusing the same read-side walk; TASK-004 wires it as a doctor advisory (non-gate) and points remediation at the fabric-review modify related-write path (review.ts:1882) — no silent hot-path edge writes. All shared-schema changes rebuild dist; tsc --noEmit is the closing gate. v1 drives age off existing created_at; content_hash + content_changed_at are DEFERRED (no sqlite persistence layer in this repo).

waves.phase: F1-wiring + F2-wiring (each depends on its wave-1 foundation)

stages: F1: config-loader reads per-type half-lives + per-maturity floor from fabric-config.json
F1: scoreDescriptionItem computes credibilityFactor(created_at age, half-life, maturity floor) and multiplies the fused score
F1: scoreBreakdownForItem mirrors the same factor so score_breakdown.credibility is emitted and final === score
F1: recall.ts surfaces score_breakdown.credibility over the MCP wire (schema-declared, survives .strip())
F2: collectStoreCanonicalEntries walks the read-set corpus (existing)
F2: suggestRelatedEdges(nodes) computes Jaccard(summary/keyword tokens) + tag-intersection + shared relevance_paths → {source,target,confidence,provenance}[] filtered at confidence >= 0.6
F2: knowledgeDoctorChecks emits a non-gate advisory listing suggested edges; remediation copy points at fabric-review modify (related write path review.ts:1882)

design_decisions: F1-v1 drives content-age decay off the EXISTING created_at field (agents-meta.ts:67, written at extract-knowledge.ts:760). content_hash + content_changed_at table columns are DEFERRED — this repo has NO sqlite persistence layer, so a per-edit content clock cannot be persisted in v1; created_at is the available age driver.

The credibility multiplier is a MULTIPLICATIVE factor (mirrors maestro-flow scoring.ts:270 `score *= credibilityFactor`), applied at the single insertion point plan-context.ts:1528 by wrapping the additive return: `return (content + structuralScaleFor(context)*structural + proximity) * credibilityFactor(item, context)`. It is eligible to scale content down (stale content sinks even on a literal match).

The multiplier has a per-maturity FLOOR (draft 0.4 / verified 0.55 / proven 0.7) so it never zeroes a match — this is the softened form of the 'content leads' invariant: proven knowledge decays more slowly, draft decays fastest. The maestro-flow permanently-unreachable `ceiling` config is intentionally NOT ported.

credibility is ORTHOGONAL to the existing recencyBoost: recencyBoost is a binary +25 ADDITIVE bump for created_at within a 7-day window (plan-context.ts:1480-1489); credibility is a continuous full-age-axis MULTIPLICATIVE decay. They compose safely without double-penalizing (verified: the +25 is inside `content+structural+proximity`, then multiplied — a fresh entry gets both the bump and factor≈floor-ceiling≈1).
credibility is ALSO orthogonal to doctor's decay: doctor decay is usage-inactivity age (last-recalled clock); credibility is content age (created_at). Different axes, no overlap.

Per-type half-life defaults (team-curated knowledge decays slower than maestro-flow's): decisions 180d / guidelines 150d / models 150d / pitfalls 120d / processes 120d. Config reader clones config-loader.ts:231 readOrphanDemoteThresholdDays' per-key validated Map pattern (integer [1,3650], best-effort hot-path-safe, corrupt config → defaults).

F2 SCOPE CORRECTION (explore-verified): the related_appended RENDER path is ALREADY complete and default-on (plan-context.ts:479-507 build → plan-context-hint.ts:231-254 assemble → knowledge-hint-broad.cjs:644 + knowledge-hint-narrow.cjs:1256 render `(related-to-<id>)`). This feature includes NO render work. The only gap is that source `related:[]` is empty (census-confirmed) — edges do not exist. F2 = auto edge GENERATION only.

F2 write routing (hard): suggestRelatedEdges does NOT write edges on the recall/archive hot path (would pollute the pure-read path, bypass the review human gate, and violate fabric-connect's 'sparse over dense'). Suggestions flow through the doctor advisory surface → the operator applies them via fabric-review modify, whose related-write path is review.ts:1882 (patch.related → flowArray). The advisory is non-gate (KT-DEC-0007) with no auto-fix arm (KT-PIT-0016), consistent with the sibling inspectRelatedGraph advisory at knowledge-doctor-checks.ts:51.

F2 confidence threshold 0.6: edges below 0.6 are dropped (not proposed) to keep the graph sparse; each suggested edge carries a provenance marker naming which signal(s) fired (token-jaccard / tag-overlap / shared-path) so the reviewer can judge.

patterns: config reader: clone config-loader.ts:231 readOrphanDemoteThresholdDays per-key validated Map (best-effort, hot-path-safe, defaults on parse failure)
score multiplier: maestro-flow scoring.ts:270 `score *= credibilityFactor` applied at the single fused-return point
final===score invariant: any change to scoreDescriptionItem MUST be mirrored component-for-component in scoreBreakdownForItem (plan-context.ts comments assert this by construction)
MCP wire schema: any new score_breakdown component MUST be declared in the api-contracts.ts zod object or .strip() drops it at the boundary (KT-PIT-0005)
pure function beside existing: suggestRelatedEdges sits next to buildRelatedGraph, factory-free, unit-testable with hand-built fixtures (no I/O)
advisory not gate: doctor knowledge checks are read-only advisories, never a gate, no auto-fix arm (KT-DEC-0007 / KT-PIT-0016)
conventions: ESM — relative imports carry the .js suffix
strict TypeScript — no any, no @ts-ignore, no as-cast to silence
after editing any shared schema (api-contracts.ts / fabric-config.ts / agents-meta.ts) run `pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-shared build` to rebuild dist before server/cli can consume it

field-deletion / rename guard: use `node includes` full-suffix round-trip census (include .cjs/.mjs/scripts/.github/workflows) — this machine's Bash grep has false-negatives, NEVER use grep as a deletion predicate
dependencies: @fenglimg/fabric-server (plan-context scoring, config-loader, doctor-related-graph, cross-store-recall, text-tokenize via shared)

_metadata.feature_branch_note: New branch; may be rebased onto / merged with feat/fabric-kb-slim (Plan A) as the follow-on wave.
deferred: content_hash + content_changed_at persisted content clock (no sqlite layer in repo; F1-v1 uses created_at instead)
Plan A feat/fabric-kb-slim items as a later wave
